zotero.abstractNote: We study the convergence of continuous-time stochastic processes for solving minimax optimization problems. Specifically, we analyze the long-term behavior of a class of stochastic differential equations (SDEs) that generalize the gradient descent-ascent (GDA) dynamics. We show that, under suitable conditions, these SDEs converge to the set of stationary points of the minimax objective. We further provide a discretization analysis, showing that the discrete-time counterparts of these SDEs, which include several popular minimax optimization algorithms as special cases, inherit the convergence properties of the continuous-time processes. Our results provide a unified framework for analyzing the convergence of a wide range of minimax optimization algorithms and suggest new, more efficient algorithms.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 27th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Statistics
